message:
fix(h3,nats,split,fault,pool): reject late frames on finished H3 streams, deduplicate NATS SubscribeGuard, simplify split waker tracking, pool waiter fairness tests

src/http/h3_native.rs:
- Track finished request streams in a BTreeSet to reject late frames
  arriving on QUIC streams after the H3 request completed, preventing
  state corruption from out-of-order QUIC delivery
- Add finished_request_streams checks in on_request_stream_frame() and
  finish_request_stream()
- Add regression test: finished_request_stream_rejects_late_frames

src/messaging/nats.rs:
- Hoist SubscribeGuard from three inline definitions into a single
  top-level struct, eliminating code duplication across subscribe(),
  request(), and consume()
- Fix JSON unescape: remove redundant explicit match arms for '"', '\',
  '/' — these are already handled by the catch-all 'other' arm

src/net/tcp/split.rs + src/net/unix/split.rs:
- Simplify wakers_changed tracking in deregister_interest: initialize
  from interest.is_readable() directly instead of setting a flag inside
  the if body, reducing branching

src/channel/fault.rs:
- Refactor reorder-buffer-full path: use block expression returning
  Option<T> instead of mutable Option + separate assignment, making the
  control flow clearer and enabling the explicit drop(buffer)

src/http/h1/codec.rs:
- Refactor obs-text fallback from match/Err branch to map_or_else for
  clippy::manual_map compliance

src/lab/meta/mutation.rs:
- Fix immutable-then-extend pattern: shadow mutations with a mutable
  binding inside the cfg block instead of declaring the outer vec as mut

src/sync/pool.rs:
- Add pool_return_wakes_waiters_one_at_a_time test verifying that
  returning one resource from an exhausted pool wakes exactly one waiter
- Add pool_timed_acquire_wakes_on_return and pool_close_wakes_all_waiters
  tests for waiter lifecycle coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
